2604.11699.title: Legal2LogicICL: Improving Generalization in Transforming Legal Cases to Logical Formulas via Diverse Few-Shot Learning
2604.11699.paper_summary: This work aims to improve the generalization of logic-based legal reasoning systems by integrating recent advances in NLP with legal-domain adaptive few-shot learning techniques using LLMs. Existing logic-based legal reasoning pipelines typically rely on fine-tuned models to map natural-language legal cases into logical formulas before forwarding them to a symbolic reasoner. However, such approaches are heavily constrained by the scarcity of high-quality annotated training data. To address this limitation, we propose a novel LLM-based legal reasoning framework that enables effective in-context learning through retrieval-augmented generation. Specifically, we introduce Legal2LogicICL, a few-shot retrieval framework that balances diversity and similarity of exemplars at both the latent semantic representation level and the legal text structure level. In addition, our method explicitly accounts for legal structure by mitigating entity-induced retrieval bias in legal texts, where lengthy and highly specific entity mentions often dominate semantic representations and obscure legally meaningful reasoning patterns. Our Legal2LogicICL constructs informative and robust few-shot demonstrations, leading to accurate and stable logical rule generation without requiring additional training. In addition, we construct a new dataset, named Legal2Proleg, which is annotated with alignments between legal cases and PROLEG logical formulas to support the evaluation of legal semantic parsing. Experimental results on both open-source and proprietary LLMs demonstrate that our approach significantly improves accuracy, stability, and generalization in transforming natural-language legal case descriptions into logical representations, highlighting its effectiveness for interpretable and reliable legal reasoning. Our code is available at https://github.com/yingjie7/Legal2LogicICL.
2604.11699.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作旨在通過將最近在自然語言處理（NLP）方面的進展與法律領域的自適應少樣本學習技術結合，來改善基於邏輯的法律推理系統的泛化能力，使用大型語言模型（LLMs）。現有的基於邏輯的法律推理流程通常依賴於微調模型，將自然語言法律案例映射到邏輯公式中，然後再將其轉發給符號推理器。然而，這種方法受到高質量標註訓練數據稀缺的嚴重限制。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了一種新穎的基於LLM的法律推理框架，通過檢索增強生成來實現有效的上下文學習。具體而言，我們介紹了Legal2LogicICL，一種少樣本檢索框架，它在潛在語義表示層面和法律文本結構層面上平衡了示例的多樣性和相似性。此外，我們的方法明確考慮了法律結構，通過減輕法律文本中由實體引起的檢索偏差來實現，因為冗長且高度特定的實體提及往往主導語義表示並掩蓋法律上有意義的推理模式。我們的Legal2LogicICL構建了信息豐富且穩健的少樣本示範，實現準確且穩定的邏輯規則生成，而無需額外訓練。此外，我們構建了一個新的數據集，名為Legal2Proleg，該數據集標註了法律案例與PROLEG邏輯公式之間的對應關係，以支持法律語義解析的評估。在開源和專有的LLM上進行的實驗結果表明，我們的方法在將自然語言法律案例描述轉換為邏輯表示方面顯著提高了準確性、穩定性和泛化能力，突顯了其在可解釋和可靠的法律推理中的有效性。我們的代碼可在https://github.com/yingjie7/Legal2LogicICL獲得。
2604.11699.author: Jieying Xue et.al.
2604.11699.authors: Jieying Xue, Phuong Minh Nguyen, Ha Thanh Nguyen, May Myo Zin, Ken Satoh
